task_completion.description: Import the historical earthquake records JSON file into TiddlyWiki.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TiddlyWiki import interface showing 'usgs_historical_earthquakes.json' being imported, or a confirmation message indicating the earthquake tiddlers have been successfully imported.
The TiddlyWiki tiddler editor is open, showing the interface for drafting a new tiddler.

task_completion.description: Set the title of the new tiddler to 'Top Earthquakes Dashboard'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title input field in the tiddler editor contains exactly 'Top Earthquakes Dashboard'.

task_completion.description: Assign the 'Dashboard' tag to the tiddler.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'Dashboard' is visible as an active tag pill in the tags section of the tiddler editor.

task_completion.description: Write a dynamic list filter for the top 5 earthquakes sorted by magnitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text body of the tiddler in the editor contains a TiddlyWiki filter expression that includes exactly 'tag[Earthquake]', '!nsort[magnitude]', and 'limit[5]'.

task_completion.description: Save and display the dashboard showing the top 5 earthquakes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The saved 'Top Earthquakes Dashboard' tiddler is displayed in view mode, showing a rendered list of exactly 5 earthquake entries.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the TiddlyWiki web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window showing the TiddlyWiki interface is visible, rather than the agent relying solely on terminal commands to manipulate files.

integrity.description: Verify the list is dynamically generated and not hardcoded.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tiddler edit screen shows TiddlyWiki filter syntax (e.g., using `<$list>` or `<<list-links>>` macros) instead of plain text, hardcoded names of the top 5 earthquakes.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't bypass the UI by editing backend files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the TiddlyWiki UI in a browser, not a text editor (like nano or vim) editing the raw TiddlyWiki HTML or JSON files directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard title is exactly 'Top Earthquakes Dashboard'. The expected tag is 'Dashboard'. The dynamic list filter must contain the exact strings: 'tag[Earthquake]', '!nsort[magnitude]', and 'limit[5]'.
